event.name: Women's 800m Final
event.venue: World Athletics Indoor Championships Kujawy Pomorze 26, Torun

commentary.pre_start: A world-class women's 800m final is loaded in Torun, with Keely Hodgkinson chasing her first world indoor title.
commentary.opening: Hodgkinson takes control early from the outside and works to stay clear of trouble as the field settles into position.
commentary.through_200: Hodgkinson leads through 200m in 27.26, with Getachew committed to the pace and Werro and Wiley tracking just behind.
commentary.through_400: The Olympic champion reaches 400m in 56.95 and keeps the race stretched, while Getachew tries to go with her.
commentary.through_600: Hodgkinson still leads at 600m in 1:26.46 as Werro and Wiley begin to close on Getachew behind her.
commentary.closing: There is no catching Hodgkinson on the final circuit as she powers away from the field.
commentary.finish: Hodgkinson wins the world title in a championship record 1:55.30, with Werro second in 1:56.64 and Wiley third in a PB of 1:58.36.

notes: Structured from official race analysis and lane assignment data supplied by the user.
This replay uses 100m split intervals from the official World Athletics race analysis.
source.writeup_excerpt: Keely Hodgkinson won in a championship record of 1:55.30 after leading through 200m in 27.26, 400m in 56.96, and 600m in 1:26.46.

event.name: Women's 800m Final A
event.venue: Crédit Mutuel - Meeting Hauts-de-France Pas-de-Calais, Liévin

commentary.pre_start: World Athletics Indoor Tour at Liévin. Keely Hodgkinson takes the start with the indoor 800m world record of 1:55.82 (Ceplak, 2002) in her sights.
commentary.opening: Pacer Anna Gryc takes the field through a fast first 100m. Hodgkinson tucks in behind.
commentary.through_200: 26.80 at 200m for Hodgkinson — on pace for sub-1:55.
commentary.through_400: 56.01 at 400m. Pacer Gryc has done her job and drops out. Hodgkinson now solo.
commentary.through_600: 1:25.06 at 600m. The world record is within reach.
commentary.closing: 200m to go. Hodgkinson holds the pace through the back stretch.
commentary.finish: 1:54.87. World indoor record. Ceplak's 24-year-old mark is finally broken.

notes: Paced race. Anna Gryc paced through 400m, then dropped out (DNF).
Hodgkinson's 1:54.87 broke Jolanda Ceplak's indoor world record of 1:55.82 from March 2002.

event.name: Men's 800m Final A
event.venue: Crédit Mutuel - Meeting Hauts-de-France Pas-de-Calais Trophée EDF, Lievin

event.name: Women's 800m Finale A
event.venue: Meeting Metz Moselle Athlélor Crédit Mutuel, Metz

event.name: Men's 800m Finale A
event.venue: Meeting Metz Moselle Athlélor Crédit Mutuel, Metz

commentary.pre_start: BAUHAUS-galan, the Wanda Diamond League in Stockholm. Pacemaker Rachel Klopfenstein will tow the field at a 1:54 target. Keely Hodgkinson, the world lead at 1:54.87, headlines; Audrey Werro sits behind her.
commentary.opening: Klopfenstein takes them out hard — 26.7 at 200m. Hodgkinson settles second, Werro tucked in just behind.
commentary.through_200: 27.0 for Werro at 200m, 27.2 for Hodgkinson. The pace is genuinely fast.
commentary.through_400: Klopfenstein through 400m in 55.6 and steps off. Hodgkinson and Werro inherit the lead at 56-flat — on world-record schedule.
commentary.through_600: 1:25.3 at 600m, Hodgkinson fractionally ahead of Werro. The rest of the field is already detached.
commentary.closing: Hodgkinson kicks off the final bend and leads into the straight — but Werro is coming.
commentary.finish: Werro storms past — 1:53.98! Diamond League record, meeting record, the third-fastest 800m in history and the first woman under 1:54 in 43 years. Hodgkinson 1:54.33 for a British record in second.

notes: Outdoor 400m track — 2 laps. All other replays to date are 200m indoor ovals.
Per-athlete 100m splits taken from the official Swiss Timing 'Race analysis' PDF.
Two-group waterfall start (lane-group in the PDF, e.g. 1-1 vs 1-2) mapped onto 8 lanes; shared lanes auto-split inner/outer by finish time.
Pacemaker Rachel Klopfenstein led through 500m (1:10.6) then dropped out (DNF).
Werro 1:53.98 broke Semenya's DL record (1:54.25) and Almanza's meeting record (1:56.28).
